frameworks.name: policy gradient reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: memory-based policy gradient model accounting for counterfactual value updates

frameworks.name: associative memory
frameworks.one_line: memory for choice-option associations is recruited to value unchosen options

core_question: What role does associative memory play in valuing unchosen (counterfactual) options during decision-making?
key_assumptions: unchosen options can still be valuated via counterfactual reasoning
association between choice options is held in episodic/associative memory
policy updates can be driven by memory rather than direct experience
manipulations: experimental interference with memory formation during choice tasks
measurement of memory for option–option associations alongside choice behavior
key_findings: an inverse decision bias is observed in valuation of unchosen options
the inverse decision bias correlates with memory for choice-option associations
memory interference reduces the inverse decision bias
a memory-based policy gradient model reproduces both the bias and its memory dependence
interpretations: associative memory plays a significant role in counterfactual valuation
decision-making and memory interact mechanistically rather than as separate systems
